query: "amyotrophic lateral sclerosis" OR "motor neuron disease" OR "motor neurone disease" OR "SOD1 ALS" OR "C9orf72 ALS" OR "TDP-43 ALS"

items.title: Therapeutic frontiers in ALS: iPSC-based drug discovery, cell therapy, and gene therapy-Advances through 2026.
items.summary: Three converging therapeutic paradigms-iPSC-based drug discovery, cell transplantation, and gene therapy-have substantially expanded the therapeutic pipeline for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) between 2020 and 2026. The FDA's accelerated approval of tofersen (Qalsody) in April 2023 marked the first treatment targeting a genetic cause of ALS. iPSC-derived drug candidates, including ropinirole and bosutinib, have completed early-phase clinical trials led by Japanese institutions. Cell therapies targeting neuroinflammation through regulatory T cells are being actively explored as immunomodulatory strategies, although efficacy remains to be established in adequately powered trials. Next-generation gene-silencing approaches-including RNA interference (RNAi) therapeutics and AAV-delivered microRNA-entered first-in-human trials in 2024-2025. The identification of STMN2 as a downstream target of TDP-43 dysfunction has opened a potential TDP-43-downstream nucleic acid therapeutic avenue for sporadic ALS, which constitutes approximately 90% of all cases, with company-reported interim data suggesting target engagement in the ongoing Phase 1/2 ANQUR trial (QRL-201). This review synthesizes the latest evidence across all three therapeutic domains, with attention to the hierarchy of evidence, regulatory milestones, and the pioneering contributions of Japanese research groups.
items.titleZh: ALS治疗前沿：基于iPSC的药物发现、细胞治疗和基因治疗——截至2026年的进展
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述总结了2020-2026年间ALS治疗的三大前沿方向：iPSC药物发现、细胞治疗和基因治疗。FDA已批准首个针对ALS遗传病因的药物tofersen；iPSC衍生药物如罗匹尼罗完成早期试验；针对TDP-43下游靶点STMN2的核酸疗法（QRL-201）正在进行1/2期试验，有望惠及约90%的散发性ALS患者。
items.insight: 该综述系统整合了ALS治疗三大前沿领域的最新进展，特别强调了日本研究团队的贡献和TDP-43下游靶点STMN2的转化潜力，为ALS研究者提供了全面的管线概览和未来方向指引。

aiRead.keyFinding: 截至2026年，ALS治疗在iPSC药物发现、细胞治疗和基因治疗三大领域取得显著进展，其中针对TDP-43下游靶点STMN2的核酸疗法（QRL-201）在1/2期试验中显示靶点结合，为散发性ALS提供了新希望。
aiRead.limitation: 作为综述，其结论依赖于所引用的原始研究，部分疗法（如细胞治疗）的疗效尚未在充分有力的试验中证实。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注ANQUR试验（QRL-201）的完整数据公布，以及iPSC衍生药物（如罗匹尼罗）的后续临床试验结果。

items.abstractZh: 三种汇聚的治疗范式——基于iPSC的药物发现、细胞移植和基因治疗——在2020年至2026年间显著扩展了肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）的治疗管线。2023年4月，FDA加速批准tofersen（Qalsody），标志着首个针对ALS遗传病因的治疗方法。由日本机构主导的iPSC衍生候选药物（包括罗匹尼罗和博舒替尼）已完成早期临床试验。通过调节性T细胞靶向神经炎症的细胞疗法正作为免疫调节策略被积极探索，但其疗效尚需在充分有力的试验中确立。下一代基因沉默方法——包括RNA干扰（RNAi）疗法和AAV递送的microRNA——于2024-2025年进入首次人体试验。STMN2被确定为TDP-43功能障碍的下游靶点，为散发性ALS（约占所有病例的90%）开辟了一条潜在的TDP-43下游核酸治疗途径，公司报告的期中数据显示正在进行的1/2期ANQUR试验（QRL-201）中实现了靶点结合。本综述综合了这三个治疗领域的最新证据，关注证据等级、监管里程碑以及日本研究团体的开创性贡献。
items.frontierRationale: 该综述发表于2026年，涵盖了截至2026年的最新进展，包括FDA批准的tofersen、iPSC衍生药物的早期临床试验以及针对TDP-43下游靶点STMN2的核酸疗法（QRL-201）的期中数据，具有高度ALS相关性和新近性。

items.title: Optimized multiplex immunofluorescent protocols for simultaneous in situ identification of α-motoneuron subtypes.
items.summary: Accurate identification of α-motoneuron (α-MN) subtypes - slow (S), fast fatigue-resistant (FR), fast fatigue-intermediate (FI), and fast fatigable (FF) - is essential for studying motor circuit organization and selective vulnerability in neurodegenerative disease. While electrophysiological approaches can distinguish these subtypes, existing immunohistochemical (IHC) methods lack the ability to simultaneously identify all four α-MN classes in situ, particularly the FI subtype, limiting their utility for large-scale or tissue-based analyses. Here, we present novel multiplex immunofluorescent strategies that enables simultaneous in situ identification of S, FR, FI, and FF α-MN subtypes, including intermediate populations, within single sections of mouse lumbar spinal cord. This approach integrates a combinatorial marker framework with optimized co-labeling conditions to resolve subtype-specific molecular signatures, including FI MNs, which have not been previously distinguishable using standard IHC methods. We establish a systematic validation pipeline demonstrating robust and reproducible subtype classification across multiple protocols, sexes, mouse strains, and disease conditions, including the G93A-SOD mouse model of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis. Labeled populations recapitulate known size distributions and exhibit consistent subtype-specific patterns across lumbar segments, supporting both the accuracy and reproducibility of the method. By enabling comprehensive in situ classification of all major α-MN subtypes, this approach represents a substantive refinement of multiplex IF, overcoming key limitations of existing IF methods and enabling analyses of α-MN subtype organization and selective vulnerability that were previously not feasible with standard histological techniques. This framework is broadly applicable to studies of motor system organization, aging, and neurodegenerative disease.
items.titleZh: 优化的多重免疫荧光方案用于同时原位鉴定α运动神经元亚型
items.summaryZh: 本研究开发了一种优化的多重免疫荧光方案，可在小鼠脊髓切片中同时区分四种α运动神经元亚型（S、FR、FI、FF），包括之前难以识别的FI亚型。该方法在ALS小鼠模型中验证了其准确性和可重复性，为研究运动神经元选择性易损性提供了新工具。
items.insight: 该研究解决了ALS研究中一个长期存在的技术瓶颈——无法在组织切片中同时区分所有α运动神经元亚型，特别是FI亚型。这使得研究者能够更精确地分析不同亚型在ALS中的选择性易损性，为理解疾病机制和开发靶向治疗提供了重要工具。

aiRead.keyFinding: 开发了能够同时原位鉴定四种α运动神经元亚型（包括FI亚型）的多重免疫荧光方案，并在ALS小鼠模型中验证了其稳健性和可重复性。
aiRead.limitation: 目前仅在G93A-SOD小鼠模型中验证，尚未在人类组织或其他ALS模型中测试；方法依赖于特定抗体组合，可能需针对不同物种优化。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注该方法在人类脊髓组织或其它ALS动物模型（如TDP-43或C9orf72模型）中的应用，以及是否可用于评估治疗干预对特定亚型的影响。

items.abstractZh: 准确鉴定α运动神经元（α-MN）亚型——慢速（S）、快速抗疲劳（FR）、快速疲劳中间型（FI）和快速易疲劳（FF）——对于研究运动回路组织和神经退行性疾病中的选择性易损性至关重要。虽然电生理方法可以区分这些亚型，但现有的免疫组化（IHC）方法无法同时原位识别所有四种α-MN类别，特别是FI亚型，限制了其在大规模或组织分析中的应用。在此，我们提出了新颖的多重免疫荧光策略，能够在单个小鼠腰髓切片中同时原位鉴定S、FR、FI和FF α-MN亚型，包括中间群体。该方法整合了组合标记框架与优化的共标记条件，以解析亚型特异性分子特征，包括之前使用标准IHC方法无法区分的FI运动神经元。我们建立了一个系统验证流程，证明在多种方案、性别、小鼠品系和疾病条件（包括ALS的G93A-SOD小鼠模型）下，亚型分类具有稳健性和可重复性。标记的群体重现了已知的大小分布，并在腰段表现出一致的亚型特异性模式，支持了方法的准确性和可重复性。通过实现所有主要α-MN亚型的全面原位分类，该方法代表了多重IF的实质性改进，克服了现有IF方法的关键限制，并使得以前使用标准组织学技术无法进行的α-MN亚型组织和选择性易损性分析成为可能。该框架广泛适用于运动系统组织、衰老和神经退行性疾病的研究。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究发表于2026年，针对ALS小鼠模型开发了创新的多重免疫荧光方案，首次实现了所有α运动神经元亚型的原位同时鉴定，具有直接的ALS转化价值。

items.title: Innate immune crosstalk in ALS/FTD pathogenesis.
items.summary: Marked by protein aggregation, impaired proteostasis, organelle stress, and chronic neuroinflammation, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and frontotemporal dementia (FTD) form a clinically, genetically, and pathologically overlapping disease spectrum. Increasing evidence indicates that innate immune activation is not merely a secondary response to neuronal injury, but an active driver of disease progression. In this review, we elaborate on how ALS/FTD-associated genetic lesions and pathogenic protein aggregates, including TDP-43, SOD1, FUS, and C9orf72-derived dipeptide repeat proteins, engage three interconnected innate immune pathways: cGAS-STING, NLRP3 inflammasomes, and TREM2-DAP12 signaling. We further highlight emerging crosstalk among these pathways, in which cGAS-STING and NLRP3 reinforce inflammatory signaling, while NLRP3-driven TREM2 shedding may impair microglial clearance and perpetuate proteostatic failure. Understanding this immune network may help define disease subtypes, identify biomarkers, and guide combinatorial therapeutic strategies that suppress harmful inflammation while preserving protective microglial functions.
items.titleZh: ALS/FTD发病机制中的先天免疫串扰
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述指出，在ALS和FTD中，先天免疫激活不仅是神经损伤的后果，更是推动疾病进展的关键因素。文章重点分析了cGAS-STING、NLRP3炎症小体和TREM2-DAP12三条免疫通路如何被致病蛋白（如TDP-43、SOD1、FUS和C9orf72）激活，并揭示了它们之间的相互增强关系，为开发针对免疫网络的组合疗法提供了理论基础。
items.insight: 该综述系统整合了ALS/FTD中先天免疫通路的最新进展，特别是cGAS-STING与NLRP3之间的正反馈环路以及TREM2脱落对微环境的影响，为研究者提供了从免疫串扰角度理解疾病异质性和寻找新治疗靶点的框架。

aiRead.keyFinding: ALS/FTD中cGAS-STING、NLRP3和TREM2-DAP12三条先天免疫通路形成相互增强的串扰网络，驱动神经炎症和蛋白质稳态失败。
aiRead.limitation: 作为综述，其结论依赖于已发表研究，缺乏原始实验数据验证；免疫串扰的具体分子机制在体内仍需进一步确认。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注针对cGAS-STING或NLRP3通路的抑制剂在ALS/FTD动物模型中的疗效，以及TREM2脱落作为生物标志物的临床验证研究。

items.abstractZh: 以蛋白质聚集、蛋白质稳态失衡、细胞器应激和慢性神经炎症为特征，肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）和额颞叶痴呆（FTD）构成了一个临床、遗传和病理上重叠的疾病谱系。越来越多的证据表明，先天免疫激活不仅是神经元损伤的继发性反应，而且是疾病进展的主动驱动因素。在本综述中，我们详细阐述了ALS/FTD相关的遗传缺陷和致病性蛋白质聚集体（包括TDP-43、SOD1、FUS和C9orf72衍生的二肽重复蛋白）如何参与三个相互关联的先天免疫通路：cGAS-STING、NLRP3炎症小体和TREM2-DAP12信号传导。我们进一步强调了这些通路之间新出现的串扰，其中cGAS-STING和NLRP3增强炎症信号，而NLRP3驱动的TREM2脱落可能损害小胶质细胞清除功能并延续蛋白质稳态失败。理解这一免疫网络可能有助于定义疾病亚型、识别生物标志物，并指导组合治疗策略，以抑制有害炎症同时保留保护性小胶质细胞功能。
items.frontierRationale: 该综述发表于2026年，聚焦于ALS/FTD中先天免疫通路（cGAS-STING、NLRP3、TREM2-DAP12）之间的新近发现的串扰机制，具有高度的ALS相关性和转化价值。

items.title: PBMC DEG/miRNA biomarkers of TDP-43 pathology in ALS.
items.summary: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) lacks reliable, disease-specific, and minimally invasive biomarkers, representing a major barrier to early diagnosis and patient stratification. The primary aim of this translational pilot study was to identify a disease-specific, TDP-43-related, gene-microRNA (miRNA) signature in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) of ALS patients with potential diagnostic value. To this end, we first identified differentially expressed disease-specific genes (dsDEGs) using a TDP-43-based rat model of ALS, generated by stereotaxic infusion of full-length (FL) TAR DNA-binding protein 43 (TDP-43) into the motor cortex. Transcriptomic profiling of the motor cortex revealed candidate dsDEGs, which were subsequently validated by RT-qPCR in motor cortex, spinal cord, and PBMCs from the same animals. To assess translational relevance, expression levels of these dsDEGs were analyzed in PBMCs from early- to mid-stage ALS patients and matched healthy controls, while disease specificity was evaluated using Parkinson's disease (PD) samples. In parallel, conserved miRNAs predicted to target the identified dsDEGs were examined in both rat and human PBMCs. Five dsDEGs, Mctp1, Penk, Mt2A, Drd1, and Rasgrp2, were consistently dysregulated across central and peripheral tissues in the TDP-43 rat model. RT-qPCR analysis of human PBMCs confirmed significant and selective dysregulation of these genes in ALS, but not in PD, supporting disease specificity. Moreover, exposure of human neuroblastoma cells and healthy PBMCs to TDP-43 recapitulated the ALS-like expression changes. Computational and experimental analyses identified seven conserved miRNAs targeting these dsDEGs, of which four were significantly downregulated in ALS PBMCs, supporting a coordinated regulatory network. Receiver operating characteristic (ROC) analyses demonstrated strong discriminative performance for both the gene signature (AUC 0.87-1.00) and the associated miRNAs (AUC 0.95-1.00). Together, these findings define a novel PBMC-based gene-miRNA signature that mirrors central ALS pathology and shows high diagnostic accuracy and disease specificity, highlighting its potential as a minimally invasive biomarker for ALS.
items.titleZh: ALS中TDP-43病理的PBMC DEG/miRNA生物标志物
items.summaryZh: 本研究在ALS患者的PBMC中发现了一组由5个基因和4个miRNA组成的特征，该特征能高精度区分ALS患者与健康对照及帕金森病患者，且与中枢TDP-43病理相关。
items.insight: 该研究提供了一种基于外周血的、疾病特异性的TDP-43病理相关生物标志物组合，有望用于ALS的早期诊断和患者分层，且其诊断效能（AUC接近1.0）非常突出，值得进一步临床验证。

aiRead.keyFinding: 在ALS患者PBMC中鉴定出5个基因（Mctp1, Penk, Mt2A, Drd1, Rasgrp2）和4个miRNA组成的诊断特征，AUC达0.87-1.00，且对ALS具有疾病特异性。
aiRead.limitation: 样本量未明确说明，且为初步研究，需要在更大、更多样化的队列中验证；TDP-43大鼠模型与人类病理的完全对应性需进一步确认。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注该基因-miRNA特征在更大规模ALS队列中的验证结果，以及其能否区分不同ALS亚型或预测疾病进展。

items.abstractZh: 肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）缺乏可靠、疾病特异且微创的生物标志物，这构成了早期诊断和患者分层的主要障碍。这项转化性初步研究的主要目的是在ALS患者的外周血单核细胞（PBMC）中识别一种具有潜在诊断价值的疾病特异性、TDP-43相关的基因-微小RNA（miRNA）特征。为此，我们首先使用基于TDP-43的大鼠ALS模型（通过将全长TAR DNA结合蛋白43（TDP-43）立体定向注入运动皮层生成）识别了差异表达的疾病特异性基因（dsDEG）。运动皮层的转录组分析揭示了候选dsDEG，随后通过RT-qPCR在同一动物的运动皮层、脊髓和PBMC中进行了验证。为了评估转化相关性，分析了早期至中期ALS患者和匹配健康对照PBMC中这些dsDEG的表达水平，并使用帕金森病（PD）样本评估了疾病特异性。同时，在人和大鼠PBMC中检测了预测靶向已识别dsDEG的保守miRNA。在TDP-43大鼠模型中，五个dsDEG（Mctp1、Penk、Mt2A、Drd1和Rasgrp2）在中枢和外周组织中一致失调。人PBMC的RT-qPCR分析证实了这些基因在ALS中显著且选择性地失调，但在PD中未发现，支持了疾病特异性。此外，将人神经母细胞瘤细胞和健康PBMC暴露于TDP-43重现了类似ALS的表达变化。计算和实验分析识别了七个靶向这些dsDEG的保守miRNA，其中四个在ALS PBMC中显著下调，支持了一个协调的调控网络。受试者工作特征（ROC）分析显示，基因特征（AUC 0.87-1.00）和相关miRNA（AUC 0.95-1.00）均具有强大的区分性能。总之，这些发现定义了一种新的基于PBMC的基因-miRNA特征，该特征反映了中枢ALS病理，并显示出高诊断准确性和疾病特异性，突显了其作为ALS微创生物标志物的潜力。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究于2026年发表，首次在ALS患者PBMC中鉴定出与中枢TDP-43病理直接相关的基因-miRNA特征，具有高诊断准确性，为开发微创、疾病特异性的ALS生物标志物提供了新方向。

items.title: Early-onset neuroinflammation drives neurodegeneration caused by lysosomal PI(3,5)P 2 insufficiency.
items.summary: Phosphatidylinositol 3,5-bisphosphate [PI(3,5)P 2 ] is a lysosomal signaling lipid whose deficiency, caused by mutations in the PIKfyve complex subunits FIG4 or VAC14, underlies a spectrum of fatal neurologic diseases including Charcot-Marie-Tooth type 4J (CMT4J) and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). To map the molecular consequences of PI(3,5)P 2 insufficiency in the brain, we performed quantitative proteomic and transcriptomic analyses of three mouse lines bearing distinct loss-of-function mutations in Fig4 or Vac14, examining the brain at the presymptomatic and end stages. Strikingly, profound neuroinflammation was already present at postnatal day 5 (before significant neurodegeneration), characterized by complement activation, interferon signaling, and parenchymal infiltration of peripheral myeloid cells and T-cells. Isolated mutant microglia exhibited a markedly pro-oxidative transcriptional state with elevated reactive oxygen species, a partly non-cell-autonomous phenotype, being present in microglia from mice with conditional Fig4 inactivation in just neurons and astrocytes. Comparison of early (P5) and late (P25) proteomics data revealed that PI(3,5)P 2 insufficiency impairs developmental remodeling of the brain proteome: proteins typically upregulated during postnatal maturation failed to accumulate, implicating lysosomal function in neurodevelopment. We identify coordinated elevation of p53, Fas receptor, inflammatory caspases, Gasdermin D, RIPK1, and ZBP1, consistent with multifactorial inflammatory cell death with features of apoptosis, pyroptosis, and necroptosis. Many of the dysregulated proteins are encoded by genes mutated in lysosomal storage disorders, ALS, CMT, Alzheimer's and Parkinson diseases, extending the pathogenic relevance of PI(3,5)P 2 insufficiency. Together, these findings establish that early neuroinflammation is a defining - and likely initiating - feature of neurodegeneration caused by disruption of lysosomal PI(3,5)P 2 .
items.titleZh: 早发性神经炎症驱动由溶酶体PI(3,5)P₂不足引起的神经退行性变
items.summaryZh: 该研究通过三种不同基因突变小鼠模型，发现溶酶体PI(3,5)P₂脂质不足在神经退行性变发生前就已引发强烈的早期神经炎症，包括补体激活、干扰素信号和外周免疫细胞浸润。小胶质细胞呈现促氧化状态，且这种炎症部分由神经元和星形胶质细胞中的非细胞自主机制驱动。研究还揭示了PI(3,5)P₂不足阻碍大脑发育性蛋白质组重塑，并激活多种炎症性细胞死亡通路。
items.insight: 该研究首次系统证明早期神经炎症是溶酶体PI(3,5)P₂不足驱动神经退行性变（包括ALS）的起始事件，而非继发后果，为靶向早期炎症干预提供了关键时间窗和分子靶点。

aiRead.keyFinding: PI(3,5)P₂不足在神经退行性变发生前即引发早期神经炎症，并激活多种炎症性细胞死亡通路。
aiRead.limitation: 研究基于小鼠模型，人类中PI(3,5)P₂不足与神经炎症的时序关系及因果性仍需验证。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注PI(3,5)P₂通路在ALS患者脑脊液或组织中的炎症标志物验证，以及靶向早期炎症的干预策略在动物模型中的疗效测试。

items.abstractZh: 磷脂酰肌醇3,5-二磷酸[PI(3,5)P₂]是一种溶酶体信号脂质，其缺乏由PIKfyve复合体亚基FIG4或VAC14突变引起，是包括Charcot-Marie-Tooth 4J型（CMT4J）和肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）在内的一系列致命性神经系统疾病的基础。为了绘制大脑中PI(3,5)P₂不足的分子后果，我们对三种携带Fig4或Vac14不同功能缺失突变的小鼠品系进行了定量蛋白质组学和转录组学分析，检查了症状前和终末期的大脑。引人注目的是，在出生后第5天（在显著的神经退行性变之前）已经存在深刻的神经炎症，其特征是补体激活、干扰素信号传导以及外周髓系细胞和T细胞的实质浸润。分离的突变小胶质细胞表现出明显的前氧化转录状态，伴有升高的活性氧，这是一种部分非细胞自主的表型，存在于仅神经元和星形胶质细胞中条件性失活Fig4的小鼠的小胶质细胞中。早期（P5）和晚期（P25）蛋白质组学数据的比较显示，PI(3,5)P₂不足损害了大脑蛋白质组的发育重塑：通常在出生后成熟过程中上调的蛋白质未能积累，暗示溶酶体功能在神经发育中的作用。我们确定了p53、Fas受体、炎症性半胱天冬酶、Gasdermin D、RIPK1和ZBP1的协同升高，与具有凋亡、焦亡和坏死性凋亡特征的多因素炎症性细胞死亡一致。许多失调的蛋白质由在溶酶体贮积症、ALS、CMT、阿尔茨海默病和帕金森病中突变的基因编码，扩展了PI(3,5)P₂不足的致病相关性。总之，这些发现确立了早期神经炎症是由溶酶体PI(3,5)P₂破坏引起的神经退行性变的一个决定性——且可能是起始性——特征。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究于2026年发表，通过多组学分析在症状前阶段揭示PI(3,5)P₂不足引发早期神经炎症的机制，直接关联ALS等神经退行性疾病的起始病理过程，具有重要的转化价值。

items.title: Drivers of Rising Prevalence in Major Motor Neurodegenerative Diseases: Temporal Trends in Sweden and France (2003-2022).
items.summary: The prevalence of Parkinson disease (PD), multiple sclerosis (MS), and motor neuron diseases (MNDs) is rising globally. However, it is unclear to what degree this is related to an increase in incidence or to improved survival after diagnosis. We performed 2 nationwide, population-based, retrospective cohort studies, including all individuals living in Sweden between 2001 and 2016 and living in France between 2009 and 2022, respectively. Pooled mixed-effects regression models, with country as a random effect, were used to determine temporal trends in prevalence, crude and age-standardized and sex-standardized incidence, and age and life expectancy at diagnosis. Annualized prevalence of PD, MS, and MNDs increased significantly between 2003 and 2022 in the pooled model (PD: prevalence ratio [PR] per year = 1.014, p < 0.001; MS: PR = 1.029, p < 0.001; MND: PR = 1.028, p < 0.001). While the crude incidence of both PD and MS remained nearly stable over time (PD: incidence rate ratio [IRR] per year = 0.998, p < 0.001; MS: IRR = 0.992, p < 0.001), the standardized incidence showed a more marked decrease for PD (IRR = 0.986, p < 0.001) while remaining almost unchanged for MS (IRR = 0.995, p < 0.001). For MNDs, both the crude and standardized incidence increased over time (crude IRR = 1.018, p < 0.001; standardized IRR = 1.008, p < 0.001). Life expectancy at diagnosis of PD increased between 2003 and 2013 (+0.95 months per calendar year, p < 0.001) and then decreased between 2013 and 2022 (-1.20 months, p = 0.002), while it increased significantly over the entire study period for MS (+2.35 months, p < 0.001) and MNDs (+0.34 months, p = 0.01). These findings indicate that the rising MS prevalence is largely survival-driven and the rising MND prevalence reflects a true increase in incidence, whereas PD prevalence grows modestly, largely independent of incidence. Depending on the mechanism that drives prevalence, whether increased incidence reflecting changing risk factor exposures, improved survival due to therapeutic advances, or demographic aging of the population, inferences about underlying causes differ substantially between PD, MS, and MNDs, with direct implications for health care planning and etiologic research.
items.titleZh: 主要运动神经退行性疾病患病率上升的驱动因素：瑞典和法国的时间趋势（2003-2022年）
items.summaryZh: 基于瑞典和法国的大规模人群研究显示，2003-2022年间运动神经元病（包括ALS）的患病率显著上升，且其粗发病率和标准化发病率均增加，提示MND患病率上升主要源于发病率的真实增长，而非单纯生存期延长。
items.insight: 该研究通过大规模人群数据区分了患病率上升的不同驱动因素，明确指出MND（包括ALS）的患病率上升反映了发病率的真实增加，而非仅因诊断后生存改善。这对ALS病因学研究（如环境风险因素暴露变化）和医疗资源规划具有直接指导意义。

aiRead.studyType: 基于人群的回顾性队列研究
aiRead.keyFinding: 运动神经元病（MND）的患病率上升主要由发病率真实增加驱动，而非生存期改善。
aiRead.limitation: 研究基于瑞典和法国数据，可能无法完全推广至其他地区；未区分MND亚型（如ALS与原发性侧索硬化）。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注后续研究是否进一步分析MND发病率增加的具体风险因素（如环境暴露、遗传背景），以及不同亚型间的差异。

items.abstractZh: 帕金森病（PD）、多发性硬化（MS）和运动神经元病（MND）的患病率在全球范围内上升。然而，这在多大程度上与发病率增加或诊断后生存期改善有关尚不清楚。我们进行了两项全国性、基于人群的回顾性队列研究，分别纳入2001年至2016年间居住在瑞典的所有个体和2009年至2022年间居住在法国的所有个体。采用以国家为随机效应的混合效应回归模型，确定患病率、粗发病率和年龄及性别标准化发病率、诊断时年龄和预期寿命的时间趋势。在混合模型中，2003年至2022年间PD、MS和MND的年化患病率显著增加（PD：每年患病率比[PR]=1.014，p<0.001；MS：PR=1.029，p<0.001；MND：PR=1.028，p<0.001）。虽然PD和MS的粗发病率随时间保持基本稳定（PD：每年发病率比[IRR]=0.998，p<0.001；MS：IRR=0.992，p<0.001），但标准化发病率显示PD下降更明显（IRR=0.986，p<0.001），而MS几乎不变（IRR=0.995，p<0.001）。对于MND，粗发病率和标准化发病率均随时间增加（粗IRR=1.018，p<0.001；标准化IRR=1.008，p<0.001）。PD诊断时预期寿命在2003年至2013年间增加（每年+0.95个月，p<0.001），然后在2013年至2022年间下降（-1.20个月，p=0.002），而MS（+2.35个月，p<0.001）和MND（+0.34个月，p=0.01）在整个研究期间显著增加。这些发现表明，MS患病率上升主要由生存期驱动，MND患病率上升反映了发病率的真实增加，而PD患病率增长温和，基本与发病率无关。根据驱动患病率的机制——是反映风险因素暴露变化的发病率增加、治疗进步带来的生存期改善，还是人口老龄化——PD、MS和MND的潜在原因推断存在显著差异，这对医疗保健规划和病因学研究具有直接影响。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究基于2026年发表的最新大规模人群数据，直接揭示了ALS等运动神经元病患病率上升的驱动因素为发病率真实增加，具有重要的ALS病因学转化价值。

items.source: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
items.title: IRE1 regulates the proteostasis of TDP-43/TARDBP in ALS/FTD through ribosome-associated quality control.
items.summary: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) and frontotemporal dementia (FTD) are progressive neurodegenerative disorders characterized by motor neuron degeneration, leading to muscle weakness, atrophy, and cognitive impairments. A defining pathological hallmark of ALS/FTD is the cytosolic mislocalization and accumulation of TAR DNA-binding protein 43 (TDP-43), highlighting its critical role in ALS pathogenesis. However, the molecular mechanisms underlying TDP-43 proteostasis remain poorly understood. Through a genetic screening approach, we identify inositol-requiring enzyme 1 (IRE1), an endoplasmic reticulum-resident transmembrane protein, as a potent suppressor of TDP-43 protein levels. Furthermore, we show that ribosome-associated quality control (RQC) factors play a crucial role in regulating TDP-43 proteostasis and cellular toxicity. Activation of the RQC pathway prevents excessive accumulation of TDP-43 and associated toxicity. Mechanistically, our findings suggest that IRE1 regulates TDP-43 protein level by promoting the degradation of aberrant TDP-43 translation product through the RQC pathway. IRE1 acts canonically to enhance the transcription of the RQC core component Clbn/NEMF and noncanonically to physically interact with Clbn/NEMF, thereby ameliorating TDP-43-induced proteotoxicity. Moreover, ectopic expression or pharmacological activation of IRE1 alleviates TDP-43 pathology and restores cognitive function in the TDP-43 A315T ALS mouse models. Collectively, our study identifies a role for IRE1 in the translational quality control of TDP-43 and establishes its potential as a therapeutic target for ALS/FTD.
items.titleZh: IRE1通过核糖体相关质量控制调控ALS/FTD中TDP-43/TARDBP的蛋白稳态
items.summaryZh: 该研究通过遗传筛选发现IRE1是TDP-43蛋白水平的抑制因子，并揭示IRE1通过核糖体相关质量控制（RQC）通路促进异常TDP-43翻译产物的降解，从而调控TDP-43蛋白稳态。在ALS小鼠模型中，激活IRE1可减轻TDP-43病理并改善认知功能。
items.insight: 该研究首次将IRE1与TDP-43的翻译质量控制联系起来，为理解TDP-43蛋白稳态失调的分子机制提供了新视角，并提出了IRE1作为ALS/FTD治疗靶点的可能性，值得关注其在药物开发中的转化潜力。

aiRead.studyType: 机制研究（动物/细胞实验）
aiRead.keyFinding: IRE1通过经典和非经典途径激活RQC通路，促进异常TDP-43翻译产物的降解，从而减轻TDP-43病理并改善ALS小鼠模型的认知功能。
aiRead.limitation: 研究主要基于小鼠模型和细胞实验，IRE1作为治疗靶点在人类ALS患者中的有效性和安全性尚需进一步验证。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注IRE1激动剂在ALS/FTD患者中的临床试验进展，以及RQC通路在其他TDP-43蛋白病中的角色研究。

items.abstractZh: 肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）和额颞叶痴呆（FTD）是进行性神经退行性疾病，以运动神经元变性为特征，导致肌肉无力、萎缩和认知障碍。ALS/FTD的一个决定性病理标志是TAR DNA结合蛋白43（TDP-43）在胞质中的错误定位和积累，突显了其在ALS发病机制中的关键作用。然而，TDP-43蛋白稳态的分子机制仍知之甚少。通过遗传筛选方法，我们鉴定出内质网驻留跨膜蛋白——肌醇需求酶1（IRE1）是TDP-43蛋白水平的有效抑制因子。此外，我们发现核糖体相关质量控制（RQC）因子在调控TDP-43蛋白稳态和细胞毒性中发挥关键作用。RQC通路的激活可防止TDP-43的过度积累及其相关毒性。机制上，我们的发现表明IRE1通过RQC通路促进异常TDP-43翻译产物的降解来调控TDP-43蛋白水平。IRE1通过经典途径增强RQC核心组分Clbn/NEMF的转录，并通过非经典途径与Clbn/NEMF发生物理相互作用，从而减轻TDP-43诱导的蛋白毒性。此外，在TDP-43 A315T ALS小鼠模型中，IRE1的异位表达或药理学激活可缓解TDP-43病理并恢复认知功能。总之，我们的研究确定了IRE1在TDP-43翻译质量控制中的作用，并确立了其作为ALS/FTD治疗靶点的潜力。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究发表于2026年，揭示了IRE1通过RQC通路调控TDP-43蛋白稳态的新机制，具有直接的ALS相关性，并提供了动物模型中的治疗验证。

items.title: Dynamic integration of skeletal muscle signals via extracellular vesicles in motor neuron diseases.
items.summary: Extracellular vesicles (EVs) are heterogenous lipid bilayer-enclosed particles secreted by virtually all cell types. They encapsulate a diverse array of bioactive molecules, including proteins, lipids, nucleic acids, and metabolites, which can be transferred to recipient cells, thereby modulating their function and phenotype. In recent years, skeletal muscle-derived EVs (SkM-EVs) have emerged as key players in the bidirectional communication between skeletal muscle and motor neurons, contributing to the establishment and maintenance of neuromuscular homeostasis. Disruptions in this intercellular signalling have been implicated in the pathophysiology of motor neuron diseases (MNDs) such as spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). In these contexts, SkM-EVs may contribute to disease progression by delivering pathogenic cargo, including misfolded proteins and aberrant RNAs, to motor neurons. A comprehensive understanding of SkM-EV biology, particularly their roles in neuromuscular communication, could offer critical insights into disease mechanisms and identify novel opportunities for biomarker discovery and therapeutic intervention. This review synthesizes current knowledge on the functional roles of SkM-EVs in motor neuron health and disease and evaluates their potential as diagnostic tools and therapeutic vectors in the context of MNDs.
items.titleZh: 运动神经元疾病中通过细胞外囊泡实现的骨骼肌信号动态整合
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述总结了骨骼肌来源的细胞外囊泡（SkM-EVs）在运动神经元疾病（如ALS和SMA）中的作用。SkM-EVs通过携带错误折叠蛋白和异常RNA等致病分子，在骨骼肌与运动神经元之间传递信号，影响神经肌肉稳态。理解这一机制有助于发现新的生物标志物和治疗靶点。
items.insight: 该综述系统整合了SkM-EVs在ALS等运动神经元疾病中的双向通讯功能，强调了其作为疾病机制研究新视角和潜在治疗载体的价值，为ALS研究者提供了从肌肉到神经元的信号传递新思路。

aiRead.keyFinding: SkM-EVs通过递送致病性货物（如错误折叠蛋白和异常RNA）促进ALS等运动神经元疾病的进展，是神经肌肉通讯的关键介质。
aiRead.limitation: 综述基于现有文献，缺乏原始实验数据，且SkM-EVs在体内的具体作用机制仍需进一步验证。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注SkM-EVs作为ALS生物标志物或治疗载体的临床前研究，以及其在神经肌肉接头中的动态变化。

items.abstractZh: 细胞外囊泡（EVs）是由几乎所有细胞类型分泌的异质性脂双层包裹的颗粒。它们包裹着多种生物活性分子，包括蛋白质、脂质、核酸和代谢物，这些分子可以转移到受体细胞，从而调节其功能和表型。近年来，骨骼肌来源的EVs（SkM-EVs）已成为骨骼肌与运动神经元之间双向通讯的关键参与者，有助于神经肌肉稳态的建立和维持。这种细胞间信号的中断与运动神经元疾病（MNDs）如脊髓性肌萎缩症（SMA）和肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）的病理生理学有关。在这些情况下，SkM-EVs可能通过向运动神经元递送致病性货物（包括错误折叠的蛋白质和异常RNA）来促进疾病进展。全面了解SkM-EV生物学，特别是它们在神经肌肉通讯中的作用，可以为疾病机制提供重要见解，并确定生物标志物发现和治疗干预的新机会。本综述综合了关于SkM-EVs在运动神经元健康和疾病中功能作用的当前知识，并评估了它们在MNDs背景下作为诊断工具和治疗载体的潜力。
items.frontierRationale: 该综述发表于2026年，聚焦于SkM-EVs在ALS中的最新机制，具有高度ALS相关性和转化价值。

items.title: Isoform-specific steric zippers drive aberrant assembly and mislocalization of shortened TDP-43.
items.summary: Prion-like domain (PrLD)-mediated aggregation and concomitant dysfunction of the essential RNA-binding protein transactive response (TAR) DNA-binding protein of 43 kilodaltons (TDP-43) is a common feature of multiple debilitating neurodegenerative disorders, including amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). However, shortened TDP-43 (sTDP-43) splice isoforms where the PrLD is largely replaced by an 18-residue carboxyl-terminal tail also contribute to ALS pathophysiology and are enriched in motor neurons. Curiously, despite lacking most of the PrLD, sTDP-43 exhibits pronounced insolubility in cells and tissue of patients with ALS. Here, we establish that the short, isoform-specific carboxyl-terminal tail of sTDP-43 confers high aggregation propensity, which is encoded by two clusters of steric zippers, and can be mitigated by short RNA chaperones. Disrupting these zippers enhances sTDP-43 solubility at the pure protein level and in neurons. Notably, these steric zippers, rather than a predicted nuclear export signal in the carboxyl-terminal tail, drive cytoplasmic mislocalization and aggregation of sTDP-43 in neurons. Thus, we define the sequence-encoded determinants of aberrant sTDP-43 assembly and provide mechanistic insights into sTDP-43 disease pathology.
items.titleZh: 异构体特异性立体拉链驱动缩短型TDP-43的异常组装和错误定位
items.summaryZh: 该研究发现，缩短型TDP-43（sTDP-43）的聚集和错误定位由其特有的羧基末端尾巴中的立体拉链结构驱动，而非传统的朊病毒样结构域。破坏这些拉链可提高sTDP-43的溶解度，短RNA伴侣也能减轻其聚集倾向。
items.insight: 该研究揭示了sTDP-43（一种在ALS运动神经元中富集的剪接异构体）聚集的分子机制，为理解ALS中TDP-43病理的多样性提供了新视角，并指出了潜在的治疗靶点（如立体拉链和RNA伴侣）。

aiRead.studyType: 机制研究（体外和细胞实验）
aiRead.keyFinding: sTDP-43的聚集和错误定位由其羧基末端尾巴中的立体拉链驱动，而非PrLD或核输出信号。
aiRead.limitation: 研究主要在纯蛋白和神经元模型中进行，尚需在ALS动物模型或患者样本中验证立体拉链的病理作用。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注后续研究是否在体内模型中验证立体拉链作为治疗靶点，以及RNA伴侣能否在ALS模型中减轻sTDP-43病理。

items.abstractZh: 朊病毒样结构域（PrLD）介导的聚集以及随之而来的必需RNA结合蛋白TDP-43的功能障碍，是包括肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）在内的多种神经退行性疾病的共同特征。然而，在ALS病理生理学中，缩短型TDP-43（sTDP-43）剪接异构体（其PrLD大部分被一个18个残基的羧基末端尾巴取代）也发挥作用，并在运动神经元中富集。奇怪的是，尽管缺乏大部分PrLD，sTDP-43在ALS患者的细胞和组织中表现出显著的不溶性。在此，我们确定sTDP-43的短异构体特异性羧基末端尾巴赋予了高聚集倾向，这种倾向由两个立体拉链簇编码，并且可以通过短RNA伴侣减轻。破坏这些拉链可提高纯蛋白水平和神经元中sTDP-43的溶解度。值得注意的是，这些立体拉链（而非羧基末端尾巴中预测的核输出信号）驱动了神经元中sTDP-43的胞质错误定位和聚集。因此，我们定义了异常sTDP-43组装的序列编码决定因素，并为sTDP-43疾病病理提供了机制见解。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究于2026年发表，直接针对ALS相关TDP-43病理的异构体特异性机制，具有高度的ALS相关性和转化价值。

items.title: Lighting Up Mislocalized Proteins: Quantum Dot Probes for Multiplexed Cytoplasm-Selective Cell Profiling in Neurodegeneration.
items.summary: Semiconductor quantum dots (QDs) provide unique stability, brightness, and multiplexed capacity for biomarker detection in complex diseases; however, their distinctive intracellular distribution has rarely been leveraged for spatially resolved diagnostics. Here, we show how QD-based sensors enable selective detection of cytoplasmic proteins and can quantify nucleo-cytoplasm protein mislocalization in patient-derived samples. We validated this approach labeling TAR DNA-binding protein 43 (TDP-43), a key mislocalized protein in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). Spatial resolution is achieved in several patient-derived models and mouse brain tissue, underscoring the nanosensor's versatility across biological systems. Multiplexed QD-based immunolabeling, combined with confocal imaging and high-throughput flow cytometry, enables the detection of distinct cytoplasmic biomarker signatures that discriminate ALS patients from healthy controls. These signatures include variations in TDP-43 mislocalization and protein coexpression patterns, which were further modulated by pharmacological treatment. This work establishes QDs as spatially selective, multiplexable nanosensors capable of resolving subtle yet disease-relevant intracellular phenotypes in patient-derived samples. Compared to organic fluorophores, QDs enhance sensitivity, improve signal stability, and enable simultaneous spatially resolved biomarker quantification, broadening their potential for clinical diagnostics and personalized medicine. These findings establish QDs as powerful tools for neurodegeneration research, disease monitoring, and early biomarker discovery, with potential applications in translational neuroscience and precision medicine.
items.titleZh: 点亮错误定位的蛋白质：用于神经退行性疾病中多重细胞质选择性细胞分析的量子点探针
items.summaryZh: 本研究开发了基于量子点的纳米传感器，能够选择性检测细胞质中的TDP-43蛋白，并量化其在ALS患者样本中的错误定位。通过多重标记结合成像和流式细胞术，可区分ALS患者与健康对照，并监测药物效果。
items.insight: 该研究为ALS研究者提供了一种新的工具，能够以高灵敏度和空间分辨率直接检测患者样本中TDP-43的错误定位，这是ALS的核心病理特征之一。量子点的多重检测能力有望推动早期诊断和个性化治疗监测。

aiRead.keyFinding: 量子点探针能够选择性检测细胞质TDP-43，并通过多重分析区分ALS患者与健康对照，且可监测药物调节效应。
aiRead.limitation: 研究主要基于患者来源细胞模型和小鼠脑组织，临床样本量有限，且量子点的长期生物相容性和体内应用仍需验证。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注该技术向临床样本（如脑脊液或血液）的转化应用，以及与其他ALS生物标志物（如SOD1、FUS）的多重检测扩展。

items.abstractZh: 半导体量子点（QDs）在复杂疾病的生物标志物检测中具有独特的稳定性、亮度和多重检测能力；然而，其独特的细胞内分布很少被用于空间分辨诊断。在此，我们展示了基于量子点的传感器如何实现对细胞质蛋白的选择性检测，并能够在患者来源样本中量化核-细胞质蛋白的错误定位。我们通过标记TAR DNA结合蛋白43（TDP-43）验证了该方法，TDP-43是肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）中一种关键的错误定位蛋白。在多种患者来源模型和小鼠脑组织中实现了空间分辨率，突显了该纳米传感器在生物系统中的多功能性。基于量子点的多重免疫标记，结合共聚焦成像和高通量流式细胞术，能够检测区分ALS患者与健康对照的独特细胞质生物标志物特征。这些特征包括TDP-43错误定位和蛋白共表达模式的变化，这些变化进一步受到药物治疗的调节。这项工作确立了量子点作为空间选择性、可多重化的纳米传感器，能够解析患者来源样本中细微但疾病相关的细胞内表型。与有机荧光团相比，量子点增强了灵敏度，提高了信号稳定性，并实现了同时的空间分辨生物标志物定量，拓宽了其在临床诊断和个性化医疗中的潜力。这些发现确立了量子点作为神经退行性疾病研究、疾病监测和早期生物标志物发现的强大工具，在转化神经科学和精准医学中具有潜在应用。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究利用量子点探针实现了对ALS患者样本中TDP-43错误定位的多重空间分辨检测，代表了纳米技术在神经退行性疾病诊断中的最新前沿应用。

items.title: A rare missense variant impacting NEK1 kinase function is associated with ALS.
items.summary: Heterozygous truncating loss-of-function (LoF) variants in NEK1 are a known cause of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS). NEK1 encodes the pleiotropic serine/threonine kinase NIMA-related kinase 1, and prior in vitro studies have implicated kinase dysfunction as the principal pathogenic mechanism underlying NEK1-associated ALS. However, bona fide pathogenic missense variants causally linked to ALS have not previously been reported, leaving this hypothesis unconfirmed. Here, we identify a rare NEK1 missense variant, p.N598S, that co-segregates with disease in a familial ALS pedigree and is enriched in European ALS cohorts. This variant exhibits normal protein expression levels, indicating a functional rather than quantitative defect. Using isogenic human motor neurons, we directly compared the effects of p.N598S with those of the ALS-associated truncating variant p.R812* to delineate disease mechanisms. The p.N598S variant induced pathological phenotypes consistent with NEK1 haploinsufficiency, including increased susceptibility to DNA damage, increased apoptosis, ciliary dysmorphia, and nucleocytoplasmic translocation of TDP-43. Importantly, p.N598S impaired NEK1 kinase activity, and pharmacological inhibition of NEK1 recapitulated the cellular phenotypes observed in both p.N598S- and p.R812*-mutant motor neurons. Collectively, these findings provide strong genetic and functional evidence for a disease-causing role of NEK1 kinase disruption in NEK1-ALS. Our findings provide immediate diagnostic and therapeutic implications, particularly for the functional interpretation of missense variants of uncertain significance and the development of targeted treatment strategies.
items.titleZh: 一个影响NEK1激酶功能的罕见错义变异与ALS相关
items.summaryZh: 本研究首次在家族性ALS家系中发现并验证了一个罕见的NEK1错义变异p.N598S，该变异通过损害NEK1激酶活性导致运动神经元出现DNA损伤、凋亡、纤毛异常和TDP-43核质转位等病理表型，证实了NEK1激酶功能丧失是ALS的致病机制。
items.insight: 该研究首次提供了NEK1错义变异与ALS因果关联的遗传和功能证据，明确了激酶活性丧失是核心致病机制，为NEK1相关ALS的精准诊断和靶向激酶的治疗策略开发提供了关键依据。

aiRead.studyType: 家族性ALS家系与功能验证研究
aiRead.keyFinding: NEK1 p.N598S错义变异通过损害激酶活性导致单倍体不足，引发DNA损伤、凋亡、纤毛畸形和TDP-43核质转位，是ALS的致病性变异。
aiRead.limitation: 研究基于一个家系和有限队列，p.N598S在散发性ALS中的频率及更广泛的NEK1错义变异谱系有待进一步验证。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注NEK1激酶抑制剂在ALS模型中的体内验证，以及针对NEK1错义变异携带者的临床试验设计。

items.abstractZh: NEK1杂合截断功能丧失变异是肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）的已知病因。NEK1编码多效性丝氨酸/苏氨酸激酶NIMA相关激酶1，既往体外研究表明激酶功能障碍是NEK1相关ALS的主要致病机制。然而，此前尚未报道过与ALS有因果关联的明确致病性错义变异，因此该假说未得到证实。本研究在一个家族性ALS家系中发现了一个罕见的NEK1错义变异p.N598S，该变异与疾病共分离，并在欧洲ALS队列中富集。该变异表现出正常的蛋白表达水平，提示为功能性而非数量性缺陷。利用同基因人运动神经元，我们直接比较了p.N598S与ALS相关截断变异p.R812*的影响，以阐明疾病机制。p.N598S变异诱导了与NEK1单倍体不足一致的病理表型，包括DNA损伤易感性增加、凋亡增加、纤毛畸形以及TDP-43的核质转位。重要的是，p.N598S损害了NEK1激酶活性，而NEK1的药理学抑制重现了p.N598S和p.R812*突变运动神经元中观察到的细胞表型。总之，这些发现为NEK1激酶破坏在NEK1-ALS中的致病作用提供了强有力的遗传和功能证据。我们的发现具有直接的诊断和治疗意义，特别是对于意义不明确的错义变异的功能解读以及靶向治疗策略的开发。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究于2026年发表，首次在家族性ALS中鉴定并功能验证了NEK1致病性错义变异，直接关联ALS核心病理TDP-43转位，具有重要的转化价值。

items.title: Learning a distance for the clustering of patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
items.summary: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) is a progressive neurodegenerative disease with median survival of 3-5 years. Patient responses to treatments vary widely, highlighting the need for personalized care. Clustering patients based on disease progression could improve prognosis, guide clinical decision-making, and optimize clinical trial design. This study aimed to identify robust ALS patient clusters using ALS Functional Rating Scale-Revised (ALSFRS-R) scores and to determine diagnostic parameters predictive of cluster membership, enabling earlier stratification and targeted management. Data from the Tours ALS center registry (April 1997-October 2023) were analyzed; after preprocessing, 353 patients monitored every three months between January 2004 and July 2023 with ALSFRS-R, clinical, biological, and demographic data were retained. After preprocessing to handle missing or aberrant data, a weakly supervised approach labeled patient pairs based on their ALSFRS-R sequences. These labels were used to train a classifier to learn a distance for off-the-shelf clustering algorithms. Multiple configurations were tested, varying clustering algorithms, dimensionality reduction method, and number of clusters. Random Forest (RF) model predicted cluster membership from diagnostic parameters. Optimal clustering was selected using silhouette score, validated with Kaplan-Meier survival analysis. Stability and robustness were assessed with the Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) and silhouette score respectively. Predictive performance was evaluated using specificity, sensitivity, positive predictive value (PPV), and negative predictive value (NPV). Diagnostic parameters associated with clusters were identified using Kruskal-Wallis and chi-squared tests for continuous and categorical variables. Three clusters (n = 139, 121, 93) were identified, demonstrating strong separation (silhouette ≈ 0.6) and high stability of results (ARI ≈ 0.7). Survival differed significantly among clusters: over 50% of patients in the third cluster survived beyond 50 months, compared to less than 25% in the other clusters. Thirteen diagnostic parameters-including ALSFRS-R subscores, IgG levels, albumin quotient, and time to diagnosis-were key predictors of cluster membership. Cluster prediction achieved specificity and NPV ≈ 0.75, with close sensitivity and PPV compared to state-of-the-art methods. This framework successfully stratifies ALS patients into clinically meaningful clusters, revealing underlying disease heterogeneity and providing strong prognostic insight. Such classification can facilitate personalized care, guide therapeutic decisions, and inform the design of targeted interventions to improve outcomes. Not applicable.
items.titleZh: 学习一种距离用于肌萎缩侧索硬化症患者的聚类分析
items.summaryZh: 本研究利用ALSFRS-R评分和弱监督学习方法，将ALS患者分为三个具有显著生存差异的聚类，并识别出13个关键诊断参数用于预测聚类归属，为ALS的个性化管理和临床试验设计提供了新工具。
items.insight: 该研究展示了如何利用常规临床数据（ALSFRS-R评分）通过机器学习方法揭示ALS的疾病异质性，其聚类结果与生存期显著相关，且预测模型具有较高的特异性和阴性预测值，为ALS研究者提供了从真实世界数据中提取预后信息并指导分层治疗的实用框架。

aiRead.studyType: 回顾性队列研究（基于临床登记数据）
aiRead.keyFinding: 通过弱监督学习，基于ALSFRS-R评分将ALS患者分为三个聚类，其生存期差异显著（第三个聚类中位生存>50个月），且13个诊断参数（如ALSFRS-R子评分、IgG水平）可预测聚类归属。
aiRead.limitation: 单中心数据（图尔ALS中心），样本量有限（353例），且需进一步外部验证；弱监督学习依赖标签质量，可能受数据预处理影响。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注该聚类方法在多中心、更大样本ALS队列中的验证结果，以及预测模型在临床试验患者分层中的实际应用效果。

items.abstractZh: 肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）是一种进行性神经退行性疾病，中位生存期为3-5年。患者对治疗的反应差异很大，凸显了个性化治疗的需求。基于疾病进展对患者进行聚类可以改善预后判断、指导临床决策并优化临床试验设计。本研究旨在利用ALS功能评定量表修订版（ALSFRS-R）评分识别稳健的ALS患者聚类，并确定预测聚类成员身份的诊断参数，从而实现早期分层和靶向管理。分析了图尔ALS中心登记数据（1997年4月至2023年10月）；预处理后，保留了353名在2004年1月至2023年7月期间每三个月监测一次、具有ALSFRS-R、临床、生物学和人口统计学数据的患者。在预处理以处理缺失或异常数据后，一种弱监督方法基于ALSFRS-R序列对患者对进行标记。这些标签用于训练分类器，以学习用于现成聚类算法的距离。测试了多种配置，包括不同的聚类算法、降维方法和聚类数量。使用随机森林（RF）模型根据诊断参数预测聚类成员身份。使用轮廓系数选择最佳聚类，并通过Kaplan-Meier生存分析进行验证。使用调整兰德指数（ARI）和轮廓系数分别评估稳定性和稳健性。使用特异性、敏感性、阳性预测值（PPV）和阴性预测值（NPV）评估预测性能。使用Kruskal-Wallis检验和卡方检验分别识别与聚类相关的连续和分类诊断参数。识别出三个聚类（n=139、121、93），显示出强分离（轮廓系数≈0.6）和高结果稳定性（ARI≈0.7）。聚类间生存率差异显著：第三个聚类中超过50%的患者存活超过50个月，而其他聚类中这一比例低于25%。十三个诊断参数——包括ALSFRS-R子评分、IgG水平、白蛋白商数和诊断时间——是聚类成员身份的关键预测因子。聚类预测的特异性和NPV≈0.75，敏感性和PPV与现有方法相当。该框架成功地将ALS患者分层为临床有意义的聚类，揭示了潜在的疾病异质性，并提供了强有力的预后见解。这种分类可以促进个性化治疗、指导治疗决策，并为设计靶向干预措施以改善预后提供信息。不适用。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究发表于2026年，采用弱监督学习结合现成聚类算法，从真实世界ALS登记数据中识别出具有临床意义的患者亚群，展示了机器学习在ALS异质性分析中的转化价值。

items.title: Intravenous administration of an engineered AAV9-gene-silencing vector suppresses human SOD1 and extends survival in an ALS mouse model.
items.summary: Adeno-associated virus (AAV)-mediated gene silencing offers a promising strategy for achieving durable therapeutic effects with a single administration. Mutations in the human superoxide dismutase 1 (hSOD1) gene, inherited in an autosomal dominant manner, lead to motor neuron degeneration in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS)-a fatal neurodegenerative disease with no effective treatment. In this study, we employed AAV9 to deliver to the SOD1 G93A ALS mouse model artificial microRNAs targeting SOD1, embedded in dual miR-33 scaffolds driven by the promoter of the human survival motor neuron 1 (hSMN1) gene. A single intravenous injection achieved widespread and sustained suppression of SOD1, preserved α-motor neurons, maintained neuromuscular junctions (NMJs), and improved muscle function. These benefits are translated into significantly improved respiratory function, motor performance, and survival. Therapeutic efficacy was observed both when the treatment was administered pre-symptomatically and during symptomatic stages. Compared with previous AAV-based interventions, the survival benefit achieved in this IV delivery approach is unprecedented, supporting its potential for clinical translation in SOD1-linked ALS and other central nervous system (CNS) diseases caused by gain-of-toxicity gene mutations.
items.titleZh: 静脉注射工程化AAV9基因沉默载体抑制人SOD1并延长ALS小鼠模型生存期
items.summaryZh: 本研究在SOD1 G93A ALS小鼠模型中，通过单次静脉注射工程化AAV9载体递送靶向SOD1的人工微RNA，实现了广泛的SOD1抑制，保护了运动神经元和神经肌肉接头，显著改善了呼吸功能、运动表现和生存期，且在症状前和症状期给药均有效。
items.insight: 该研究展示了静脉注射AAV9基因沉默载体在ALS小鼠模型中前所未有的生存获益，为SOD1相关ALS的临床转化提供了强有力的支持，值得ALS研究者关注其递送效率和治疗窗口。

aiRead.studyType: 动物实验（SOD1 G93A ALS小鼠模型）
aiRead.keyFinding: 单次静脉注射AAV9基因沉默载体可广泛抑制SOD1，保护运动神经元和神经肌肉接头，显著延长生存期，且症状前和症状期给药均有效。
aiRead.limitation: 该研究基于小鼠模型，结果需在人类临床试验中验证；长期安全性和免疫原性未评估。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注该AAV9基因沉默载体在SOD1相关ALS患者中的临床试验进展，以及其在中枢神经系统其他毒性获得性突变疾病中的应用潜力。

items.abstractZh: 腺相关病毒（AAV）介导的基因沉默为单次给药实现持久治疗效果提供了一种有前景的策略。人超氧化物歧化酶1（hSOD1）基因的常染色体显性遗传突变会导致肌萎缩侧索硬化症（ALS）中的运动神经元变性——这是一种尚无有效治疗的致命性神经退行性疾病。在本研究中，我们使用AAV9将靶向SOD1的人工微RNA（嵌入由人运动神经元存活基因1（hSMN1）启动子驱动的双miR-33支架中）递送至SOD1 G93A ALS小鼠模型。单次静脉注射实现了广泛且持续的SOD1抑制，保护了α-运动神经元，维持了神经肌肉接头（NMJs），并改善了肌肉功能。这些益处转化为呼吸功能、运动表现和生存期的显著改善。在症状前和症状期给药时均观察到治疗效果。与既往基于AAV的干预相比，本静脉注射方法实现的生存获益是前所未有的，支持其在SOD1相关ALS及其他由毒性获得性基因突变引起的中枢神经系统（CNS）疾病中的临床转化潜力。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究利用工程化AAV9载体通过静脉注射实现高效基因沉默，在ALS小鼠模型中取得前所未有的生存期延长，具有直接的ALS相关性和临床转化价值。

items.title: Karyoptosis mediates cell death and neurodegeneration upon proteotoxic stress.
items.summary: Neurodegenerative diseases are frequently associated with proteotoxic stress linked to disease specific proteins. The autophagy-lysosome system provides essential control of proteotoxic stress and its failure can lead to initiation of apoptosis. However, in aging and neurodegenerative diseases apoptosis is insufficient to account for all neuronal death, and several different cell death types have been reported in these contexts. Here we show that karyoptosis, a distinct form of cell death, can be induced by proteotoxic stress and then develops through nuclear degeneration and cellular expulsion of nuclear material. We establish that karyoptosis is regulated by the p38 kinase signalling pathway, which controls stability of the nuclear lamina protein LaminB1 via direct phosphorylation. We demonstrate that karyoptosis affects neurons in models of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis/frontotemporal dementia (ALS/FTD) pathology. Finally, we identify karyoptotic features in post-mortem frontal cortex of FTD and Alzheimer's disease (AD) patients. Together these findings characterise a form of cell death directly linked to proteotoxic stress and nuclear lamina stability that is associated with neurodegeneration.
items.titleZh: 核凋亡介导蛋白毒性应激下的细胞死亡与神经退行性变
items.summaryZh: 研究发现一种新的细胞死亡形式——核凋亡，由蛋白毒性应激触发，通过p38激酶通路调控LaminB1稳定性，在ALS/FTD模型和患者脑组织中均有发现，为神经退行性变提供了新的细胞死亡机制。
items.insight: 该研究首次系统描述核凋亡在ALS/FTD中的作用，揭示了p38-LaminB1轴作为潜在治疗靶点，为理解蛋白毒性应激导致的神经元死亡提供了全新视角，值得ALS研究者关注。

aiRead.studyType: 基础研究（细胞与动物模型）结合临床样本观察
aiRead.keyFinding: 核凋亡是蛋白毒性应激诱导的新型细胞死亡形式，受p38-LaminB1通路调控，在ALS/FTD和AD中均有证据。
aiRead.limitation: 研究主要基于细胞和动物模型，临床样本仅来自死后组织，需进一步验证核凋亡在活体患者中的动态过程。
aiRead.watchNext: 关注p38抑制剂或LaminB1稳定剂在ALS/FTD动物模型中的治疗效果，以及核凋亡标志物在患者体液中的检测可行性。

items.abstractZh: 神经退行性疾病常与疾病特异性蛋白相关的蛋白毒性应激有关。自噬-溶酶体系统对蛋白毒性应激提供关键控制，其功能失效可导致凋亡启动。然而，在衰老和神经退行性疾病中，凋亡不足以解释所有神经元死亡，且在这些背景下已报道了多种不同的细胞死亡类型。我们在此展示，核凋亡（一种独特的细胞死亡形式）可由蛋白毒性应激诱导，并通过核变性和细胞核物质排出而发展。我们确定核凋亡由p38激酶信号通路调控，该通路通过直接磷酸化控制核纤层蛋白LaminB1的稳定性。我们证明核凋亡影响肌萎缩侧索硬化/额颞叶痴呆（ALS/FTD）病理模型的神经元。最后，我们在FTD和阿尔茨海默病（AD）患者的死后额叶皮层中识别出核凋亡特征。这些发现共同表征了一种与蛋白毒性应激和核纤层稳定性直接相关、并与神经退行性变相关的细胞死亡形式。
items.frontierRationale: 该研究于2026年发表，首次在ALS/FTD模型中鉴定核凋亡这一新型细胞死亡机制，具有高度ALS相关性和转化价值。
